name: First National Bank In New Bremen
description_short: First National Bank in New Bremen is a community bank chartered in 1934, serving individuals, businesses, and organizations across five Ohio locations with traditional banking and investment services.
description_long:
First National Bank in New Bremen was established in 1934 as a national bank and has grown to operate five branch locations across northwestern Ohio: New Bremen (headquarters), Wapakoneta, Sidney, New Knoxville, and Botkins. The bank maintains traditional community banking roots while offering modern digital services to its customer base.

The bank provides a comprehensive suite of personal and business banking services including checking and savings accounts, personal and business loans, home equity products, and credit cards (Visa). On the investment side, First National Bank offers CDs, IRAs, money market accounts, financial planning, stocks and bonds, mutual funds, retirement accounts, and annuities. They also feature mobile banking with real-time transaction notifications, geographic spending restrictions via GPS, and customizable security preferences.

First National Bank distinguishes itself as a locally-rooted community bank with specific branch managers and customer service managers assigned to each location. The institution emphasizes security and consumer protection, offering resources on identity theft prevention, fraudulent charge disputes, and account security. Their dual focus on personal and business banking, combined with investment services typically found at larger institutions, sets them apart from smaller community banks.

As a regional community bank, First National Bank offers personalized service and local decision-making, but customers should note that the bank's digital infrastructure and product breadth may be more limited compared to larger national banks. The institution appears stable and well-established but does not advertise specialized products like wealth management or investment advisory beyond basic financial planning services. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of deposit (CDs) with published rates
pros: Five branch locations across Ohio provides convenient local access in New Bremen, Wapakoneta, Sidney, New Knoxville, and Botkins
Mobile banking app with real-time transaction notifications, GPS-based spending restrictions, and customizable security settings
Established institution with 90+ years of history, chartered as a national bank in 1934
Full-service offerings including loans, investments, CDs, IRAs, mutual funds, and financial planning in one institution
Extended Saturday hours (8:30 AM - 12:00 PM) in addition to weekday service
Dedicated customer service managers and branch managers listed for each location enabling personalized service
Visa credit card offerings for both personal and business customers
cons: Limited to five small-town locations in Ohio only, not suitable for customers outside this region
No evidence of digital-only or neobanking features; appears to be traditional brick-and-mortar focused
Saturday hours limited to half-day (12:00 PM closing), no Sunday service mentioned
Website content does not specify competitive rates or product-specific details like APY on savings or loan terms
No mention of specialized wealth management, trust services, or investment advisory offerings

contact.address: 435 S Washington St, New Bremen, OH 45869

best_for: Small business owners in northwestern Ohio seeking local relationship banking and business lending
Individuals in rural Ohio communities (New Bremen, Wapakoneta, Sidney, New Knoxville, Botkins) seeking full-service banking
Customers prioritizing in-person service and local decision-making over digital-only banking
Individuals seeking integrated banking and investment services from a single community institution
diagnosis: First National Bank in New Bremen is best for small business owners and individuals in its five Ohio service areas seeking relationship-based community banking with integrated investment services. The main caveat is limited geographic availability and a traditional banking model that may lack the digital sophistication or specialized products of larger institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 435 S Washington St, New Bremen, OH 45869
